- `IRetrievalService` that takes a user query string
- Embed the query ‚Üí vector similarity search against `DocumentChunk` table
- Return top-K (5) chunks above a minimum similarity threshold (0.75)
- Include source attribution (document name, chunk index)

- `IChatService` orchestrating the RAG flow
- Build system prompt with retrieved context chunks
- Call `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync` for streamed responses
- Persist messages to `ChatMessage` table
- Manage chat sessions (create, list, load history)

### 7. Blazor UI
- `MainLayout` with sidebar for chat session list
- `Chat.razor` page ‚Äî message thread + input box
- Real-time streaming display of LLM responses
- "New Chat" button, session switching
- Source attribution display (which docs were referenced)
- Loading indicators and error handling

### 8. DI registration & Program.cs wiring
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register EF Core context with Npgsql
- Register all application services
- Configure middleware, static files, Blazor endpoints

### 9. Database migration & startup
- Create initial EF Core migration
- Auto-apply migrations on startup (dev) or provide migration script
- Enable pgvector extension in migration

### 10. Testing & validation
- Verify `dotnet build` succeeds cleanly
- Verify the ingestion pipeline processes PDFs into chunks
- Verify the RAG retrieval returns relevant results
- Verify the Blazor UI renders and streams responses

## Guardrails Applied

- **Temperature**: Set to 0 for factual documentation Q&A
- **MaxOutputTokens**: Set explicitly (1024)
- **Retry logic**: Use `RetryingChatClient` wrapper
- **Secrets**: API key from env var / user-secrets only
- **Model pinning**: Use specific dated model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- **RAG relevance threshold**: Minimum similarity score of 0.75
- **Source attribution**: Always show which document chunks informed the answer
- **Streaming**: Use `IAsyncEnumerable` for time-to-first-token
- **Embedding caching**: Never re-embed already-ingested content

- Postgres with pgvector must be available at runtime (connection string in config)

## Technology Stack

| Component | Technology | NuGet Package |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| Web UI | Blazor Server (Interactive) | built-in |
| LLM abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` |
| LLM provider | OpenAI | `OpenAI` |
| Vector store | pgvector via MEVD | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` + Npgsql pgvector connector |
| Document ingestion | PDF text extraction + custom chunking | `UglyToad.PdfPig` |

| Embedding generation | Via MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator` | included in `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` |

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add all required NuGet packages to the .csproj
- Configure the project for Blazor Server with interactive server rendering

### 2. Configuration & secrets
- Add `appsettings.json` structure for Postgres connection string, OpenAI settings, docs folder path
- Use `IOptions<T>` pattern for typed configuration
- API keys loaded from environment variables / user-secrets (never committed)

### 3. Data models & EF Core context
- `ChatSession` entity (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` entity for vector store (Id, DocumentName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding)
- `AppDbContext` with migrations, pgvector extension enabled

### 4. PDF ingestion service
- `IDocumentIngestionService` that scans the `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text from each PDF using PdfPig
- Chunk text into ~500-token segments with overlap
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into Postgres (skip if already ingested)
- Trigger on app startup (or via admin endpoint)

### 5. RAG retrieval service
- `IRetrievalService` that takes a user query string
- Embed the query ‚Üí vector similarity search against `DocumentChunk` table
- Return top-K (5) chunks above a minimum similarity threshold (0.75)
- Include source attribution (document name, chunk index)

- `IChatService` orchestrating the RAG flow
- Build system prompt with retrieved context chunks
- Call `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync` for streamed responses
- Persist messages to `ChatMessage` table
- Manage chat sessions (create, list, load history)

### 7. Blazor UI
- `MainLayout` with sidebar for chat session list
- `Chat.razor` page ‚Äî message thread + input box
- Real-time streaming display of LLM responses
- "New Chat" button, session switching
- Source attribution display (which docs were referenced)
- Loading indicators and error handling

### 8. DI registration & Program.cs wiring
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register EF Core context with Npgsql
- Register all application services
- Configure middleware, static files, Blazor endpoints

### 9. Database migration & startup
- Create initial EF Core migration
- Auto-apply migrations on startup (dev) or provide migration script
- Enable pgvector extension in migration

### 10. Testing & validation
- Verify `dotnet build` succeeds cleanly
- Verify the ingestion pipeline processes PDFs into chunks
- Verify the RAG retrieval returns relevant results
- Verify the Blazor UI renders and streams responses

## Guardrails Applied

- **Temperature**: Set to 0 for factual documentation Q&A
- **MaxOutputTokens**: Set explicitly (1024)
- **Retry logic**: Use `RetryingChatClient` wrapper
- **Secrets**: API key from env var / user-secrets only
- **Model pinning**: Use specific dated model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- **RAG relevance threshold**: Minimum similarity score of 0.75
- **Source attribution**: Always show which document chunks informed the answer
- **Streaming**: Use `IAsyncEnumerable` for time-to-first-token
- **Embedding caching**: Never re-embed already-ingested content

## Notes

- The `Docs/` folder already exists with a `product-guide.pdf` placeholder
- Postgres with pgvector must be available at runtime (connection string in config)
- The solution keeps everything in a single project for simplicity; can be split later
- No Agent Framework needed ‚Äî this is retrieval + single-turn generation, not agentic

## HelpChatbot ‚Äî RAG Chatbot Plan

**Approach:** A **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)** pipeline using Blazor Server for the UI.

**How it works:** PDFs from `Docs/` are ingested ‚Üí chunked ‚Üí embedded ‚Üí stored in **Postgres + pgvector**. On each user query, relevant chunks are retrieved via vector similarity and injected into the LLM prompt, which streams back an answer with source attribution.

**Key technology choices (per .NET AI guidance):**

| Layer | Choice |
|---|---|
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) |
| LLM provider | OpenAI (pinned model version) |
| Vector store | pgvector via `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` |
| PDF parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` |
| DB/ORM | EF Core + Npgsql |
| UI | Blazor Server (interactive, streaming) |

**No Agent Framework needed** ‚Äî this is single-turn retrieval + generation, not agentic.
